title: gladiolus humilis
intro: Gladiolus humilis, also known as the small gladiolus or narrow-leaved gladiolus, is a perennial plant species native to South Africa, characterized by its slender stems and small, funnel-shaped flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, and has been used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Gladiolus humilis include its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and act as an antioxidant. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the roots or leaves in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
